methods.method_name: Sample Collection and In Situ Measurements
methods.description: Collection of water samples from various lentic ecosystems and measurement of environmental parameters to study the effects of seasonality and lake characteristics.

steps.description: 200 samples were collected from different lentic ecosystems between 2006 and 2017: 99 samples from soda pans, and 101 samples from freshwater lakes in Hungary and Germany.
steps.searchable_summary: collect water samples from lentic ecosystems

steps.description: Water temperature (°C), pH, conductivity (lS cm -1), and dissolved oxygen (mg l -1) were measured in situ using a portable multimeter.
steps.searchable_summary: measure water temperature, pH, conductivity, and dissolved oxygen using a multimeter

methods.description: Determination of chemical concentrations in water samples using spectrophotometric methods.

steps.description: In the laboratory, the concentrations of dissolved inorganic nitrogen (DIN, as the sum of nitrite, nitrate and ammonium, mg l -1), total phosphorus (TP, lg l -1) and soluble reactive silica (SRSi, mg l -1) were determined using spectrophotometric methods, according to APHA (1998).
steps.searchable_summary: determine DIN, TP, and SRSi concentrations using spectrophotometry

methods.description: Collection, preservation, and identification of diatom communities from various substrates for ecological and morphological analysis.

steps.description: Benthic diatom communities were collected from the most characteristic substrates (mud in soda pans, stone in Lake Stechlin and reed in other lakes).
Samples were preserved in ethanol and prepared using the hot hydrogen-peroxide method; the diatom valves were then embedded in a mounting resin.
steps.searchable_summary: preserve and prepare diatom samples using hot hydrogen-peroxide method

steps.description: At least 400 diatom valves were identified at species level under a light microscope and the use of up-to-date taxonomic books.
steps.searchable_summary: identify diatom species using a light microscope

methods.description: Classification of diatom species by ecological and morphological traits and analysis of their distribution and niche parameters.

steps.description: Each species was classified by trait, employing diatom ecological guilds and morphological traits on the basis of their biovolume and length/width ratio.
steps.searchable_summary: classify diatom species by ecological and morphological traits

steps.description: RO and mean LA were calculated for each diatom trait.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate RO and mean LA for diatom traits

steps.description: NP and NB for each trait and their combinations were determined by outlying mean index (OMI) analyses.
steps.searchable_summary: perform OMI analyses to determine NP and NB

steps.description: Monte-Carlo test using 1,000 random permutation was implemented for statistical significance.
steps.searchable_summary: perform Monte-Carlo test for statistical significance

steps.description: Hellinger transformation for diatoms, and further transformations for environmental variables were carried out.
steps.searchable_summary: apply Hellinger transformation to diatom data

steps.description: For the principal component analyses (PCA), the environmental parameters were standardized.
Linear regression models were run to examine the relation between the niche parameters, the distributions and the mean LA of traits.
steps.searchable_summary: run linear regression models on niche parameters and trait distributions

steps.description: Variation partitioning was employed to reveal the importance of individual and shared effects of the three explanatory variables on the regional distribution of the different traits.
steps.searchable_summary: perform variation partitioning on explanatory variables
